item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be found under any source as cc-compliant. Thus, it is assumed that the image is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There seems to be something tonally off about parts of this scip. Some of the wording is a bit flowery and not really clinical enough. Other parts come off as more storytelling than dispassionate cataloguing. Also I don't understand how this is Keter as it will inevitably wipe itself out in fairly short order as it drives fennic foxes to extinction. In fact I wouldn't be surprised if that was the approach the Foundation took. Actively wipe out the population to prevent spread or mutation. Overall not a fan of this one but I don't dislike it enough to -1.
Edit: just realised this must have been changed to Euclid over its edits and I was thrown by the discussion posts. Still pretty sure this is a self resolving problem though that could quickly become neutralised.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I'm cool with this

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as described below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Coming up with a crosslink here proved a little challenging until I was pointed in the direction of SCP-367. The fact that both anomalous creatures have similar methods of entrapping prey and similar disguise methods would probably make any Johnny Foundation curious as to a potential shared origin or ancestor. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

If SCP-1294 sees a subject in this state, SCP-1294 will lie on, or against the subject, and rest. During this time, SCP-1294's skin will appear to melt, and grow over the subject, later shifting it into SCP-1294's abdomen. SCP-1294 has shown no limit to what it can envelop, and is capable of processing over 190 metric tons of food in one week. SCP-1294 only consumes living prey in this manner, preferring to eat already dead subjects or processed food normally.

To this:

If SCP-1294 sees a subject in this state, SCP-1294 will lie on, or against the subject, and rest. During this time, SCP-1294's skin will appear to melt, and grow over the subject, later shifting it into SCP-1294's abdomen. SCP-1294 has shown no limit to what it can envelop, and is capable of processing over 190 metric tons of food in one week. SCP-1294 only consumes living prey in this manner, preferring to eat already dead subjects or processed food normally1.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Similarities between SCP-1294 and SCP-367's predatory technique and choice of disguise have been noted. Investigation into a shared origin or ancestor for both anomalies is ongoing.

item.title: Anyways
item.content:encoded:
I have been working on my own cannon based upon this idea.
Welcome to project Kerberos… https://goo.gl/ZMqGDj

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it wrong that I'm getting Little Prince vibes from this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was about to point that out! I got to this SCP through pages labeled vulpine after reading the other three.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fungal date rape fennec. Not exactly something I would have guessed I'd read today. Or ever.

By the way, that makes 3/4 fox skips involving with horrific sex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what happens if you try to inseminate another animal species with the fungus

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what does the fox say?..nothing because ITS A FRICKIN LIVING FUNGUS!!!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would this picture be any better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I could have taken it more seriously if it weren't the first result for "Fennec Fox" google image search.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reverted to it's non-Cthulhuy form. Basically, I used a Cipher my friends and I commonly use for our D&D sessions. Might use it again in a tale or something, as it looks pretty lovecraftian. Hope you all had a great April 1st! Ɛ:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ph'nglui Mglw'nafh Cthulhu R'lyeh wgah'nagl fhtagn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Since the last update was today, I'm guessing it's totally cereal.

Looks like someone found a fantasy language translator.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fuck?

Is… is this the April Fools thing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wvyalaaeir Ai'lfaelalyiylf Vuai'aykguayw

What is this I officially do not even, at all, in any way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
picture

Aww!

SCP-1294 is composed entirely of a fungal material, which replicates its skin, skeleton, and fur. The majority of SCP-1294's internal structure consists of a thick mold

nn-nnno

item.title: Re: New content
item.content:encoded: Ok, this just got a big update. Added what it's like being high with it, and made some other changes. If you're wondering what happened to that thing I was going to draw, I dropped it, and decided that describing it was much easier.

item.title: New content
item.content:encoded: Alright, I think it's ready, but before I make it official and stuff, I need to 1) make the picture (you'll see if you read it) and 2) get your approval. So, I'd apreciate it if you'd PM me with what you think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not really sure what to think about this. While the core concept isn't necessarily bad, it didn't really do much for me. A fungal fox that makes people hallucinate for its own gain seems sort of uninspired to me - you went the obvious route of feeding/mating and, while that isn't at all a bad direction to take it, it left me wanting more than that. While the mating sort of touches on more by making it squicky and probably agonizing, it didn't do enough to pull me completely into the concept.

I also think your organization could use a bit of work.

SCP-1294 is composed entirely of a fungal material, which replicates its skin, skeleton, fur, and internal organs.

This seems more important than the behavioral analysis, but if you really feel the need to separate the big anomaly from the fluff-description that usually comes with the first paragraph, I'd suggest sticking the sentence in the beginning of the second one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright, bit of a progress update: I am currently working on fleshing out this thing's concept, mainly going into more detail on the noxious gas it produces via adding more test logs. I'll post a link here soon, but I feel I should tackle that first before I make significant changes; mainly because I can already tell that I'm going to have to edit a few things in the description, and I would proffer to have a nice list of things to do than taking each thing as they pop out at me.

I greatly appreciate the feedback, and just wanted to say that I'm paying close attention to your comments. So, thanks for posting. It really does help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw the picture, upvoted, then read it and am reconsidering.

If the toxin is airborne, then the obvious solution is to wear hazmat suits, not expose personnel to the toxin in <2-minute windows.

Agreed on the fact that there's no reason for it to have an animal-like internal structure.

The documentation of the female fox's last days needs serious work on tone. The visuals are described vividly, but not clinically.

(As a side note, I'm morbidly curious as to whether its gametes are more spore-like than sperm-like.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I gotta admit, I was a little squicked out there. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
with sparse black spots sparsely placed

Is there a way to replace one of those 'sparce'?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the fundamental idea. I don't like the mating-with-foxes-and-impregnating-them-with-chestbursters part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it a little bit better, but still not enough to make me upvote it. I'll withdraw my downvote and leave it neutral.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm… Well, fixed the easiest issues… I think I have some ideas for more test logs. Give me a few.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed mainly. Thanks for that. I'll take a look at the test logs when I get home. I was thinking that the foundation would either introduce an animal big enough to it (like a blue whale), or it would use a regenerative SCP to make infinite SCP burgers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's nothing here that seems to merit Keter classification, especially if it can only mate with other foxes of the same species.

It's not badly written as whole, but the fungus children trope is just a bit dull (and I've seen it done better in Mushishi). I would've liked to see some more interesting experiments. How does this euphoria affect the brain, what drug or substance is it comparable to (not all psychedelic substances are created equal)? How do people behave under the influence, and what evolutionary purpose does that serve for the anomaly? What on earth does it look like when it grows all over a human/prey— why does it have a stomach (and lungs—in fact, why does it have mammalian internal structure at all)?

Also

under the guise of local poachers

just seems a bit odd. "Local" poachers? As in poachers everyone knows? Why so specific? And from the instructions, I can't help but imagine these guys running around the Sahara torching fennec foxes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I squeed. Anyways:

If left exposed for longer than approximately two minutes, subjects will enter a consistently increasing state of euphoria
If exposed to SCP-1294 for longer than five minutes, subjects will enter a state of euphoria

You said the same thing twice within sentences of each other. It sounds clumsy. Especially if you said the same thing twice within sentences of each other.

and is capable of processing 190 metric tons of food in one week.

Is the Foundation even rationally able to give this thing 190 tonnes of food in one week? That could feed a small country.

and did not appear to be affected by the toxious environment.

Is toxious a word? 'Cause I don't think it's a word.

Subject experiences an epileptic shock

Epileptic seizure would be more appropriate. I'd also like to note that the description in the test log about the reproduction process is muddy and uncertain.

This thing doesn't feel like Keter to me. If worse comes to worst, all fennec foxes become walking MDMA scratch-n-sniffs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made fixes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm? Oh, they are entirely male because they are essentially leaching off the normal fennec fox species. So basically, SCP-1294 + normal female fennec fox = hundreds of SCP-1294 instances.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for pointing that out. Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sold on the Keter classification here. Theoretically, if all instances are male and the female is killed upon giving birth, there is a negative birth rate, so they might kill off all those foxes, but there is a finite possibility of instances if they do that. And they seem pretty easy to kill.

I like the idea, but I'm no vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1294 then proceeded to mate with the subject once unconscious.

The syntax here is a bit sloppy. I mean, it's obvious that "once unconscious" refers to the unfortunate D-Class, but since SCP-1294 is the grammatical subject, the sentence doesn't read very well.

Otherwise, this is creepy and adorable as all hell. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. Regarding the containment proceedures, pretty much yes. They state what box they need to be in, and what people need to do. Regarding the poachers, they are fairly common in the saharra. Although I find it kinda sad, fennec foxes actually have pretty valuable fur. Seeing men hauling them into a truck isn't that much of a big deal out there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's very tempting to +1 just based on the adorable picture, but will resist until I read it.
e: Okay, read it, liked it, have nitpicks.
I'm not entirely sure, but I think the "SCP" part of the title refers to the specific orders followed to maintain containment. Correct me if I'm wrong.
Also why would the MTF take the cover of poachers? They can administer amnesiacs if people see them kill one of the little guys. Maybe "Safaris, Cameras, and Photos," a Tourist trap that sells, well, safaris, cameras and photos? something like that maybe, because poachers really aren't the best cover.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Point. I'll have Mobile Task Force Xi-8 on it soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh. Site policy is to just delete drafts, as the draft system shouldn't be used. Their own damn fault for messing with unused slots that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The effects, i got that part now. Reread it and noticed it. I'd still figure that since this is going to be considered keter that an mtf be associated with it and in the containment procedures. It can also not be my cup of tea, but having a threat like this be along the lines of, 'oh hey yea let us know if you hear about any more…' That just seems lacking thus my downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah. That draft was there when I got here. Didn't know what to do with it, so I did that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There was a draft already in place and rather than overwrite it, he turned it into the first version for posterity/use by the original author, THEN wrote his own on top.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm confused by your initial creation comment. What do you mean, it isn't yours?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The possibility of offspring are highly likely due to its high birth rate. Any reports of abnormally large foxes and/or fungi in the area should be brought to the site's designated head researcher for investigation.

Subjects… behave in a similar manner to those under the influence of a psychedelic hallucinogen.

Edit: although, if it isn't your bag of course, there's little I could do about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
High-birth rate: SCP-1294 reproduces via infecting foxes, causing them to essentially give birth to fungal trees, while SCP-367 reproduces via mitosis.

Can consume massive amounts of material: SCP-1294 can only eat meat in this way, which is a VERY rare event which can pretty much only happen if it encounters a beached blue whale… in the Sahara. SCP-367 can eat everything, which plays more into its file.

Something that is something else: Yes, but that’s like, an eighth of every animal SCP ever made. If you’re talking about it being the same thing, I could point you to several SCPs that resemble cute animals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's fox fungus? Is this the only known fungus out there? Is there no mtf out there looking for more instances of fungi to prevent a wide spread breeding? What type of euphoria do human subjects encounter when around the fungus? It's too much cute but deadly for me. This seems like something a a furry scientist would keep around for too long and die from exposure. It doesn't resonate for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: High birth rate, can consume massive amounts of material, cute animal that's really made of something else ("unicellular" amoeba-esque thing vs fungus).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not seeing a resemblance, outside of the high birth-rate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Other than being a fox, this seems incredibly similar to SCP-367, right down to the exponential growth.

item.title: So. Author comments...
item.content:encoded: Damn those ears are big. Anyway, posted it in chat, and people said they liked, so here goes nothing. I sincerely hope you all enjoy.
